name: get-unpublished-changes
tagline: Diffs HEAD against the latest published npm versions and drafts a layered, human-readable changelog with version-bump recommendations.
verdict: Clean, low-risk skill: read-only git/npm inspection, no secrets, no network exfil, no destructive commands, no hidden instructions. Weaknesses are quality-of-life only: no negative triggers, no failure-path guidance (missing tag, no prior publish, npm lookup failure), no ambiguity examples for multi-layer files, and no declared tooling/version metadata in fr

highlights.summary: No negative-trigger guidance — e.g. what to do for a repo with no npm publish history, no git tags, or a non-npm project; a user could invoke this on an inapplicable repo with no guardrail.

Steps are largely actionable (explicit `git diff v{published-version}..HEAD` command, line 27) but step 1 ('Detect latest published versions') gives no concrete command (e.g. `npm view <pkg> version`)
No handling described for likely failure paths: no published version found (first release), missing git tag, or npm lookup failure — the skill assumes happy-path data availability throughout.
